cases.input: Our agent loops forever or stops as soon as it writes 'all done'; we gate on scanning reply text for 'done'/'task complete'. Fix the loop control.
Devs ignore our automated reviewer because it flags safe code as 'potential security issue' about 1 in 5 times. How do we make it trustworthy again?
Our extraction step sometimes returns prose instead of the JSON object we need. How do we guarantee a typed structured result?
We put 'never run rm -rf' in the system prompt but the agent still occasionally tries destructive commands. Where should this rule actually live?
When we spawn a research subagent it inherits the whole parent conversation and gets confused. How do we give it a clean slate and its own model?
Our classifier's accuracy looks fine on average but we suspect one document type is dragging it down. Is this a criteria problem or a sampling problem? Pick the right kata.
Can you recommend which GPU instance type to rent for fine-tuning a vision model on AWS?
Compare deterministic-agent-loop and validation-retry-feedback side by side and tell me which to use for my retry-on-bad-JSON case.
Our long-running agent forgets the critical 'always ask before deleting' rule once the conversation gets long. What pattern fixes this?
Apply the prefix-caching kata to our 40k-token static system prompt and show how to read cache_creation vs cache_read tokens, with proper evidence tags.
